steps.title: Identify condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
For a system to have infinitely many solutions, the two equations must be identical. That means both the slope and y-intercept of the second equation $ y = mx + b $ must match the first equation $ y = \frac{2}{7}x + 3 $. $$\text{Matching slope: } m = \frac{2}{7}, \quad \text{Matching y-intercept: } b = 3$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
